samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">Data width of FIFO.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">Depth of FIFO.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <para>1. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule.</para>
    <para>2. Use waddr, raddr to access dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>3. Use wclk to control the write operation of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>4. Use rclk to control the read operation of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>5. Use wenc to enable the write operation of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>6. Use wdata to provide data to the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>7. Use renc to enable the read operation of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>8. Use rdata to get data from the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>9. Use wptr to control the write pointer of the FIFO.</para>
    <para>10. Use rptr to control the read pointer of the FIFO.</para>
    <para>11. Use wptr_buff to get the previous write pointer.</para>
    <para>12. Use rptr_buff to get the previous read pointer.</para>
    <para>13. Use wfull to indicate the FIFO is full.</para>
    <para>14. Use rempty to indicate the FIFO is empty.</para>
    <para>15. Use waddr_bin to get the write address.</para>
    <para>16. Use raddr_bin to get the read address.</para>
    <para>17. Use waddr to get the write address for dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>18. Use raddr to get the read address for dual-port RAM.</para>
    </implementation>
    <task>
    <para>1. Write data to the FIFO when winc is high and wfull is low.</para>
    <para>2. Read data from the FIFO when rinc is high and rempty is low.</para>
    <para>3. Write and read data at the same time when winc and rinc are high and wfull and rempty are low.</para>
    <para>4. Write data when winc is high and wfull is high, the data will be stored in the FIFO when the next rinc is high and wfull is low.</para>
    <para>5. Read data when rinc is high and rempty is high, the data will be output when the next winc is high and wfull is low.</para>
    </task>
</module>
